video_title: Workers at St George's Colliery, Tyldesley (1901)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Miners; Child labour; St George's Colliery; Tyldesley | locais: Tyldesley | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:U5aTBtcDqX34k_kCwiriezPHC3z9cboo | Coalmining. | Edwardian miners are filmed leaving their Lancashire pit at the shift's end. | Mitchell & Kenyon more commonly filmed outside textile mills or engineering factories, so it's interesting to see them in a mining town. This film also sheds light on their techniques for filming departing workers. Several separate 'takes' capture varying combinations of men and boys exiting the site under the filmmakers' supervision. What shots reached the screen we'll probably never know.
video_title_display: Workers at St George's Colliery, Tyldesley (1901)

video_title: Workforce Leaving Alfred Butterworth and Sons, Glebe Mills, Hollinwood (1901)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Women workers; Child labour; Hollinwood; Alfred Butterworth & Sons | locais: Hollinwood | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:hlOTBtcDq4h8yjp1I4xlDbcmxE8USTBB | Note: The opening frames show a very sunny day with strong shadows; some women carry their shawls. In later frames the shadows are less strong. It was exhibited for Hollinwood and Oldman Wakes Fair. | A flood of Lancashire cotton workers and their children at the end of another shift. | Filmed for Hollinwood Wakes week in July, this good-natured but chaotic melange of adults and children is typical of a 'factory gate' film. While the ragged and mischievous boys draw attention to themselves by playing up to the camera, it's the appearance of two better-dressed young girls - one wearing ear-muffs, the other wearing spectacles, that will catch most modern eyes. | Although many of the children would have been employed as labour at the mill, the younger children can be seen carrying baskets and parcels of food which would be brought to a mother or father at the end of their morning shift.
video_title_display: Workforce Leaving Alfred Butterworth and Sons, Glebe Mills, Hollinwood (1901)

video_title: Workforce Leaving Smith & Coventry Works, Ordsall Lane, Salford (c.1901)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Salford; Ironworks | locais: Salford | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:RiODBtcDqVym21DZGIB4TLhYwU6SgN2m | Men and lads spill out at the end of their shift at a Lancashire iron works. | While the men rush out to get home after a hard day's labour, boys stop and show off for the camera, despite getting in the way of their elders. Soon the wonder of the camera attracts a larger audience - probably at the invitation of one of Mitchell and Kenyon's showman partners. Some of the men, and a passing young girl, join the throng. Close by, another young lad eagerly sells newspapers. | Smith and Coventry of Gresley, established at Ordsall Lane in 1859, manufactured machine tools. The company later moved to new premises in Timperley, Manchester, and was taken over by John Hetherington and Sons in 1929.
video_title_display: Workforce Leaving Smith & Coventry Works, Ordsall Lane, Salford (c.1901)

video_title: Workforce at Horrocks Miller & Co. Preston (c.1901)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Preston; Factories; Cotton (textile); Textile industry; Horrocks, Miller & Co. | locais: Preston | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:poYTBtcDq8qW9K6x1D8B23b2CYORD3Ru | Workers leaving Horrocks Miller & Co. cotton mill in Preston. | A vibrant impression of dinner hour at an Edwardian cotton mill. | Preston children carry lunch baskets for their working parents or siblings in a routine event made living history by the presence of Mitchell & Kenyon's camera. This film was commissioned to be shown at the local Whitsuntide fair. The Preston Guardian regretted the displacement of the fair's "wild beast, ghost, and marionette shows" by such novelties as "a pretentious cinematograph exhibition".
video_title_display: Workforce at Horrocks Miller & Co. Preston (c.1901)

video_title: Workforce of Pollock's Caledonian Cabinet Works, Beith (1901)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Child labour; Beith; Pollock's Caledonian Cabinet Works | locais: Beith | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:ZuNjBtcDoLbIM28FkCdxIhVtGHMdCdEK | A surprisingly calm 'factory gate' film, shot outside a factory in the Ayrshire town, famous for its wooden furniture making. | Seen beside some of Mitchell and Kenyon's more typical 'factory gate' films - energetic, crowded and urban - this spacious, small-town example, shot outside a much more compact workshop, is a real breath of fresh air. As workers amble down the semi-rural street, the cameraman, perhaps impatiently, stops and starts the camera. Lots of cheeky wee boys liven things up with their sociable scrapping.
video_title_display: Workforce of Pollock's Caledonian Cabinet Works, Beith (1901)

video_title: Workforce of Scott & Co. Shipyard, Greenock (1901)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Scott & Co.; Greenock | locais: Greenock | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:dkNjBtcDrUqFqVLXIPYUtZ7m3Bp1VSxl | Ship building/naval factory gate. | Unruly shipworkers defy direction from Mitchell and Kenyon's showmen partners. | Like theatre, this film begins with Scott's huge gate sliding open to disgorge its workers. But what follows is less well-rehearsed... The vain efforts of three well-dressed showmen to direct the crowd make entertaining viewing. One cheeky showman doffs his hat to the camera as he leaves on a cart. Keep an eye out for the young girl rescued just in time to avoid being mown down by a passing cart.

video_title: Workpeople Leaving For Dinner, Robinson's Iron Foundry, Rochdale (1901)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Horse-drawn transport; Rochdale; Robinsons Works | locais: Rochdale | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:lreXlscDoxOdMWmDIgzmNhilkWXNvnAQ | Note: Engineering factory gate. | Employees exit a Lancashire works on a drizzly day. | Two showmen busily leaflet for the show where this film would have been screened, and seem to have co-opted some of the kids hanging around in mid-screen... Workers head off both ways, filmed from a distance (with no sky in the frame). A second shot is taken from a different angle, the workers now cleared. The man being carried by pony trap is clearly a local VIP: perhaps the foundry boss.
video_title_display: Workpeople Leaving For Dinner, Robinson's Iron Foundry, Rochdale (1901)

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: School Children; Hats; Leeds; Terraced houses; Teachers (religious sect) | locais: Leeds | som: Silent | duração: 3 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:Bjd3lscDpvcp0okW71x3h0K43WgaYVZP | Schoolchildren file past the camera. | A small battalion of Yorkshire schoolchildren lines up for a playground photograph. | Resembling an animated school photo, this film is a remarkable record of the last days of the old Victorian 'school boards' system, and vividly captures the slightly militaristic atmosphere of early 20th century primary education: backs straight, chins up! The film is in remarkable condition, with every child's face as sharp and bright as it would have appeared to audiences in 1901. | Watch out for the gawping lad on the extreme right of the frame. Notably older than the other children, he may have been employed for the day by Mitchell and Kenyon's cameraman to stand as a marker, helping the teachers to steer their classes to the benefit of the camera.

video_title: 20,000 Employees Entering Lord Armstrong's Elswick Works (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Employees; Buses; Horse-drawn transport; Newcastle-upon-Tyne; Armstrong's Works | locais: Elswick | som: Silent | duração: 3 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:I1cjdtcDqL87C4EVqM_gyZ960pyrzenm | Factory gate film held in the Mitchell and Kenyon Collection. | An astonishingly crowded Victorian street reveals the vast scale of Tyneside manufacturing. | The men leaving Armstrong's works and other Newcastle sights are full of life, and create much excitement amongst the audience, reported Newcastle's Evening Chronicle at a screening of this film, in a mixed programme including Boer War and Boxer Rebellion scenes. The film was advertised as showing the men entering, not leaving the works, but either way, it's an impressive sight. | Some rather scrappy shots of parade, park and street scenes precede these magnificent main images. The film was commissioned and screened by prolific showman AD Thomas. You'll easily spot two of his men ushering parts of the enormous, almost all-male crowd... Lord Elswick was one of the great nineteenth-century industrialists who, incidentally, died the month after this film was made. He was by then no longer closely involved in running the Armstrong Whitworth company and its massive works, which fed many sectors of Britain...
video_title_display: 20,000 Employees Entering Lord Armstrong's Elswick Works (1900)

video_title: 1st Volunteer Battalion, East Lancashire Regiment - Blackburn Rifle Volunteers (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Guns; Lancashire; Bicycles; Military bands; Military parades; Musicians; Volunteer units; Cycles; Boer War (1899-1902) | locais: Blackburn | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:BqcjdtcDrdOD_udw5uononx0vDPQ_LlN | BOER WAR. Note - there are trees and buildings in the background, young boys walk alongside the parade. | An impressive display of numbers from the local volunteer force as part of the Blackburn Easter Fair. | The call to form volunteer army reserves in 1859 met a huge response in Lancashire, and Blackburn was no exception. The volunteers were an important part of Britain's South African War forces, becoming increasingly consolidated and professional. The diverse ages of the privates and officers is interesting - as is the small cycle corps which heads the march, outnumbering the horses.
video_title_display: 1st Volunteer Battalion, East Lancashire Regiment - Blackburn Rifle Volunteers (1900)

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Children; Women; Employees; Miners; Men; Mansfield | locais: Mansfield | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:pwYjdtcDowDvgr90HraX5wVJ1VmCfoYD | Factory Gate: Coalmining. | Grimy miners head home at the end of their shift in the days before pithead baths. | Scenes from the pithead as the miners depart after their shift. Creswell Colliery was active from 1897 and a model village was built for the miners, with houses, a co-operative store, a miners' institute, a recreation area and a bandstand. The colliery closed in 1991. Nearby Langwith Colliery was active from 1876 to 1978. It's not clear which of the two mines is shown in this film. | Both Creswell and Langwith are in Derbyshire, while Mansfield is in Nottinghamshire. However the two villages - and their pits - lie close to the Nottinghamshire border, so it may be that Mitchell & Kenyon shot these images during a visit to Mansfield.

Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Child labour; Internal combustion engines; Crossley's; Openshaw | locais: Manchester | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:pyMzdtcDpClc88IVlF--MkVvpizO2Ai7 | Note: Crossley Brothers was founded by Francis and William (later Sir) Crossley at Great Marlborough Street, Manchester in 1866. In 1882 they moved to bigger premises in Pottery Lane, Openshaw, east Manchester. By 1869 they had acquired world (except German) rights to the patents of Otto and Langden of Cologne which included the Otto four-stroke cycle. Design began on a four-stroke cycle to the gas engine resulting in the introduction of a horizontal Crossley Otto gas engine. The Crossley name has not been used since 1998 but over 100,000 Crossley oil and gas engines have been built. | This Manchester 'factory gate' film has one mysterious addition... | This single roll of film interestingly starts with a brief tranquil shot, perhaps taken to test the camera and shooting conditions. The film proper is suitably spirited, unruly and full of human details, with the operator apparently encouraging interaction with the camera. But why is a bag being dangled above the workers? It seems to puzzle some of those present as much it does we viewers today.

Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Engineering; Lincoln; Robeys Works | locais: Lincoln | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:5lNjdtcDrMD1tfnRaXZontXzZxxJKg31 | Engineering factory. Factory Gate. | Hoards of workers funnel through the great arched entrance to Robey's works in Lincoln. | The sea of male faces fleeing these Lincoln engineering works reminds us that, unlike the textile industries which relied on female and child labour, engineering remained a male domain. The group of girls in the foreground are probably meeting their fathers or brothers at dinner time. Robey's, established 1854, produced winding gear for coal mines, as well as industrial and agricultural machines.

video_title: Employees at Walker Engineering Works, Wigan (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Wigan | locais: Wigan | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:BhNjdtcDrAXUvRDiWskE8TyfM1fP81cB | Ironfounders and engineers head home after morning shift at Pagefield Works. | A damp, overcast Autumn day made life difficult for Mitchell and Kenyon's camera, and with poor focus, too, it's hard for us to read the expressions of these workers from Walker Bros, a mining and general engineering company. On cue from the cameraman, the first group of men rush forwards as if in a race, followed at a more leisurely pace by their workmates, many carrying lunch boxes and flasks.
video_title_display: Employees at Walker Engineering Works, Wigan (1900)

video_title: Employees at White Cross & Co. Ltd Wire Works, Warrington (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Warrington; Whitecross & Co Ltd | locais: Warrington | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:swNjdtcDo5R3amruWZgAfbkxdob8CeP_ | Home time for grateful workers in a Cheshire factory. | A mainly young workforce pours out of the Works Entrance of a factory. This Cheshire town grew during the Industrial Revolution and wire manufacture was well established: Whitecross dates back to 1864. Look out for the boy who approaches the camera waving his right hand in apparent imitation of the hand-cranking movements of the cameraman. As ever, the camera provokes unruly behaviour.
video_title_display: Employees at White Cross & Co. Ltd Wire Works, Warrington (1900)

video_title: Employees of Galloways Ltd. Boiler Works, Hyde Road, Manchester (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Factories; Manchester; Boilers; Child labour; Galloway's Limited | locais: Manchester | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:w1dTZtcDpW25ajuGEFXM7WhBo1XMgoEX | Engineering factory gate. | Workers surge through the great gates of a Victorianengineering works. | Some of these employees would have surely enjoyed spotting themselves when the film was projected at the local Gorton Wakes a few days later. A contemporary Gorton Reporter review conveys the popularity of these films: "The local scenes, as usual, were the great draw... shouts of approval were frequently heard as some well-known person or the features of someone in the audience were recognised". | Galloways, established by brothers William and John Galloway in 1835, specialised in the manufacture of boilers. This site was located on Hyde Road near to the original Manchester City football ground; the northeastern end of the stadium was known as the 'Galloway End'.
video_title_display: Employees of Galloways Ltd. Boiler Works, Hyde Road, Manchester (1900)

video_title: Employees of J & E Waters, Talbot Mills, Ellesmere St., Manchester (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Manchester; Women workers; J. and E. Waters | locais: Ellesmere St, Manchester | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:93dDZtcDqSSXCd4hFEowkDX4hqAZESn3 | Cotton factory gate. | The lasses, and a few lads, surge out of a Victorian cotton mill after their shift. | Unusually for the Mitchell and Kenyon collection, this film shows more of the building than the people: the epic factory arch dwarfs the workers as they hurry homewards, many of them distracted by the filmmakers. The darkness of the film may be due to the condition of the original negative. The four-storey Talbot Mill building still provides industrial accommodation in Hulme, central Manchester.
video_title_display: Employees of J & E Waters, Talbot Mills, Ellesmere St., Manchester (1900)

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Employees; Lincoln | locais: Lincoln | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:5oczZtcDo4kA1Ju0MrATHEYwwF5SO-9n | Factory gate. | Victorian industrial workers leave their factory after a demanding day. | Glimpses of a line of washing lend a picturesque air to these scenes of flat-capped workers in heavily soiled work-clothes dashing home. By 1900, Lincoln's heavy engineering industry was flourishing and it's likely these workers were building locomotives or producing agricultural machinery at one of the many companies based in the city at the time. The name of the factory remains a mystery.

video_title: Final of International Cup at Cork Regatta (1902)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Steamers; Boats; Rowing boats; Regattas; Boat racing; Cork (city); River banks | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:k5cTZtcDqk5WuixigEOTI5Q0WWff9WSa | International Gig Races at Cork International Regatta, held in Cork. Crowds watch rowing race from river bank. | Scenes from the summer final between the Leander and Berlin rowing clubs. | Mitchell and Kenyon's cameramen could usually be relied on to position themselves for the perfect shot. Alas, not this time. From the bank of the River Lee, the camera manages to catch the boats as they pass, but is too far away to pick up much detail, and keeps losing the action. Finally, the hats and waving arms of enthusiastic spectators, and a pillar, almost completely block our view. | Leander, founded in Henley-on-Thames in 1818 and one of the world's oldest rowing clubs, overcame their German rivals to become competition winners. Another film in the Mitchell and Kenyon collection, Two-oared Boat Race, Sunday's Well, Cork, captures part of the International Gig Races held during the same July event.
video_title_display: Final of International Cup at Cork Regatta (1902)

video_title: Great Northern Railway Works at Doncaster (1901)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Documentary | assuntos: Factory workers; Bridges; Doncaster | locais: Doncaster | som: Silent | duração: 5 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:kwcDZtcDrjI16GhZLqeIMO09nK0in0wd | Men and boys leaving a factory in Doncaster. | Countless men, women and children spill out on to the screen from "The Plant" at the heart of the Yorkshire railway town. | This is Mitchell & Kenyon at their best. Some near-perfect staging (bridge against buildings against sky) first frames high-spirited boys tearing towards us with infectious joy. Then the crowd thickens with older, male, blue-collar workers. Later, we see smaller groups of office workers, female as well as male. The scale and local supremacy of Doncaster's railway industry is inescapable.
video_title_display: Great Northern Railway Works at Doncaster (1901)

video_title: Howarth's Egerton Mill, Ordsall Lane, Salford (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Street scenes; Factory workers; Factories; Cotton (textile); Salford; Women workers; Haworth's Egerton Mill | locais: Ordsall Lane, Salford | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:5zbjZtcDpLx_sag9QhBpeF9vLTUui2lV | Cotton factory gate. Workers leaving a cotton mill in Salford. M&K 21 is of the same subject. Note: Because of its unusual distance from the gate, this makes a good example of a street scene. | The dinner time hustle and bustle of a Salford street at the dawn of the 20th century. | The Egerton Mill was owned by cotton giants Richard Howarth & Sons. At its height Howarth's employed around 4000 people, many of them women and young girls, in their cotton mills and weaving sheds across Salford. Unlike other Mitchell and Kenyon 'factory gate' films, which were concerned with capturing as many workers as possible, this title also offers a wonderful long-shot of Ordsall Lane.
video_title_display: Howarth's Egerton Mill, Ordsall Lane, Salford (1900)

video_title: Mill Workers Leaving Oldroyd & Sons Mill, Dewsbury (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Women workers; Wool; Textile industry; Dewsbury; Oldroyd's Mill | locais: Dewsbury | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:N0cjFtcDoofw57wj4aAeZEcbmrNBDJeb | Wool factory Gate. | Workers of all ages depart Oldroyd's wool mill – and the filmmakers' camera is a subject of curiosity. | This film was screened at the annual Dewsbury Feast in July 1900, to much approval. Said one local paper: "the living picture holds the field. To see ourselves as others see us was the leading thing, and we were met with the alluring notice that there was on view scenes outside the mills." These were provided by "the Cinematograph, the Biograph, the Electric Graph, or some other graph."

The Bankside Mill is now demolished. | Victorian cotton workers leave for their lunch break. | The cotton town of Stalybridge, near Manchester, gave a warm reception to Mitchell & Kenyon's camera, as the local Herald newspaper reported: "The mill hands... had been photographed specially for this show, and we are enabled to see the employees as they appeared for dinner. Much amusement is caused by the exclamation of the audience as one after another appears on the screen and is recognised."

video_title: Phantom Ride on the Furness Railway (1901)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Railway journeys; Furness | locais: Furness Abbey | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:FtbDBtcDrLWhhi4scaNkv0ZXfF94EXsx | A driver's view of the Furness Railway through Furness Abbey Tunnel and Station. | This picturesque railway tour opens with a fleeting glimpse of the ruins of Furness Abbey (on the left) before continuing through a tunnel past the grand railway hotel and on through the ornate Furness Abbey station. A platform-full of passengers, including a well-dressed lady cyclist and her family, stand frozen as the train passes - perhaps bemused by its refusal to stop... | The station at Furness Abbey was opened in 1846 and began receiving passengers from further afield in 1862. Services called at Furness Abbey to allow passengers to use the Furness Abbey Hotel, a converted 17th century manor house purchased by the railway company in 1847. The 12th-century Furness Abbey was once one of the wealthiest and most powerful Cistercian monasteries in the country. Hymned by William Wordsworth in his Prelude and sketched by JMW Turner, its ruins remain a popular tourist attraction.
video_title_display: Phantom Ride on the Furness Railway (1901)

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Brass bands; Parks; Gardens; Brass instruments; Accrington | locais: Accrington | som: Silent | duração: 3 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:ZudTdwcDofADvU1IeiEwai_rs-xzlRY2 | Accrington locals parade in their Sunday best. | These well turned-out locals are clearly enjoying being captured by Mitchell and Kenyon's camera as they parade through Accrington Park, though we don't know what occasion they're marking. A shot of a small child on the grass was no doubt staged by the filmmakers to elicit a tender response from audiences when the film was shown a few days later at local venues.

video_title: Ropner & Co., Shipbuilders, Stockton on Tees (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Shipbuilding; Employees; Stockton on Tees; Ropener and Co. | locais: Stockton-on-Tees | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:xoYzBtcDqsKnJ_EInFRSPyZFs9V30NOg | Shipyard factory gate film. | Hometime for thousands of Teesside shipbuilders. | This film was shown at Stockton-on-Tees' Exchange Hall, and Mitchell & Kenyon's advertising promised "magnificent animated pictures" of "10,000 Employees Leaving Stockton Shipyards." The filmmakers typically enticed their audiences with the prospect of seeing themselves on screen and for this reason they tried to include as many people in shot as possible, though there may not be 10,000 here...
video_title_display: Ropner & Co., Shipbuilders, Stockton on Tees (1900)

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Comedy | assuntos: Greetings cards; Valentine's day | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:hqampndTooiJv35CX6TOfjBEOMGIUIDq | COMEDY. An old maid receives what she thinks is a Valentine. | Early comedy in which a cruel joke is played on an old maid | The eponymous old maid in this early comic film, seated in the parlour with her cat, receives an envelope addressed to Miss Pimple. A calendar on the wall tells us it's February 14th. Could it be a valentine? Love at last? Her expression indicates that she thinks it is, until the note is opened to reveal a picture of a baby and the legend "just like Mama". Oh dear.

video_title: Workers Brooks and Doxey, West Gorton Works, Manchester (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Child labour; Brooks and Doxey; Gorton; Animal drawn Transport | locais: Manchester | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:l1aDBtcDpPs24HNk_SpCGJXoLjuIvcyC | Some infectious youthful high spirits outside an engineering factory in Victorian Manchester. | A star-struck adult who should know better tries to attract the camera's attention, but to no avail: this film belongs to the lively children and teenagers, workers or otherwise. Mitchell and Kenyon would return to Brooks and Doxey Ltd, a firm that produced machinery for the textile industry, the following year for an even better 'factory gate' production.
video_title_display: Workers Brooks and Doxey, West Gorton Works, Manchester (1900)

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Factories; Bamford's; Moseley | locais: Moseley | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:Z1ZjBtcDp4DbTzdb2EsRP1Wuh-U_rIzD | Factory gate. | The all-male workforce heads home from a factory in Victorian Birmingham. | Bamford's is believed to have been a glass manufacturer, perhaps in Moseley in Birmingham, or perhaps in Mossley in Manchester. Unusually for films in the Mitchell and Kenyon collection, the workforce is all male. Most of the men glance towards the camera and briskly continue on their way - though this may be a consequence of incorrect film speed rather than the men's walking pace.

Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Employees; Butterley | locais: Ripley | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:VvZjBtcDptEpwSXbJwTGwwdHnq4xil0n | Workers leaving the (unseen) ironworks at Butterley, Ripley, Yorkshire. | Very early footage of a vast industrial site in Ripley, and the folk who manned the machines. | One of the earliest films in the Mitchell & Kenyon collection, this features one of the most important UK engineering companies of its time. The Butterley Forge and Wagon works, founded in 1790 by Benjamin Outram, was a vast industrial site which followed the course of the Cromford Canal from the village of Stoneyford all the way to Golden Valley in Derbyshire. | Shot on 6 October 1900 for screening at the annual fair, the film shows workers who produced industrial goods such as cast-iron and steel rails, including the 'mechanical traveller', which hauled trams along the new tramways. Numerous monuments to Butterley's expertise still stand today, including the roof of St. Pancras Station and many bridges worldwide.

Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Women workers; Textile industry; Haslams; Colne (town) | locais: Colne | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:93ZDBtcDrk-V1X4PxcInYYGuP-ljWX6W | Cotton factory gate. Note: The day is very sunny, there are strong shadows but evidence of snow on the path. | Hungry and cold, Lancashire cotton workers trek home through the snow at dinner time. | When this film was made, Colne in Lancashire was home to 55 cotton mills and the Haslam Brothers Spring Gardens Mill, featured here, was one of 27 located in the Waterside area of the town. The low vantage point gives a good view of the works and hill beyond. The female workers fend off the January cold with their thick woollen shawls while a few mischievous youngsters throw snowballs. | The snow confirms the January 1900 date inscribed in the original negative, making this the oldest surviving actuality footage shot by filmmakers Mitchell & Kenyon. Perhaps egged on by the filmmakers, one young man livens the scene with a snowball fight. Unusually, Mitchell & Kenyon made a second visit to Haslam's in Colne the following year.

Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Horses; Blackburn; Draught horses; Nuttall & Co. | locais: Blackburn | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:twYzBtcDoRF1qtD9yxQTeR8I7a3BADj1 | Filmed at a production plant for Edwardian ale, thisunique footage carries a powerful scent of malt and hops. | An unusual Mitchell and Kenyon factory film: both bitter and mild. Smoke belches in the background as the class makeup of Nuttall's (later Lions') Lancashire brewery bubbles to the surface: its white collars, blue collars and aprons. Carriages pull in with barrels for bottling and crates for onward transportation. All very sober, but one lad thumbs his nose at us... Cheers!

video_title: Workers Leaving a Factory in Leyland (c.1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Women workers; Employees; Children's games and play | locais: Leyland | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:E4aDBtcDoa-w5T2kpaPvKhmWDvyhMTW- | Male and female workers leaving a factory. | Another shift ends in Victorian Lancashire, while children play in the street. | The factory is unidentified and out of sight, but the men and women form a long stream, leaving no doubt where they've come from. The cobbled street is awash with children, who gather before the camera, mesmerised. One child holds a parasol, another a wooden hoop, though both are too distracted to play. A smartly-dressed showman in bowler hat hands out leaflets advertising the film's screening.
video_title_display: Workers Leaving a Factory in Leyland (c.1900)

video_title: Workers at Bradley Shed, Nelson, Lancashire (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Children; Factory workers; Hats; Child labour; Underprivileged children; Nelson (Lancashire) | locais: Nelson | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:BtajBtcDp8Y19U5g6vSNBS81_oiKdWC- | Young cotton workers leaving a Lancashire cotton factory. | Athletic young Lancashire cotton weavers show off outside their Victorian mill. | These young cotton weavers clearly delight in the presence of Mitchell and Kenyon's camera. This animated atmosphere would have pleased the showmen, who almost certainly encouraged the boys' antics. The roly-poly and the handstand, especially, would have amused audiences when the film was shown at a 'Living Pictures' exhibition a few days later, at nearby Burnley Town Hall.
video_title_display: Workers at Bradley Shed, Nelson, Lancashire (1900)

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Lancashire; Victorian Britain (1837-1901); 1900-1909; Dickens and Heywood; Middleton | locais: Middleton | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:JjajBtcDqMV3HE9LV_dJ0aQGZQ0zXUIE | Lancashire textile factory gate film. The Middleton-based Dickens and Heywood mill specialised in the manufacture of polished yarns. Note: Image quality at the beginning of the film is poor due to decomposition. | A rare snapshot of the daily routine of the workers of a Lancashire textile mill. | Judging by the range of expressions, being filmed leaving work was either a welcome diversion or an unwanted intrusion for these work-weary Lancastrians. The film was shot in advance of a Middleton 'wakes' holiday, when the film would have been exhibited at a travelling cinematograph stand. The Middleton-based Dickens & Heywood mill specialised in the manufacture of polished yarns.

Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Factories; Cotton (textile); Textile workers; Textile industry; Stockport | locais: Stockport | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:phajBtcDoC8zqBEH5kj8gIMGSkl0kDZn | Workers leaving India Mill cotton factory in Stockport. | Spinners and weavers jostle through the exit of one of Stockport's largest textile mills. | At its height the India Mill employed around 1500 workers, many of them children. When this film was made, though, the cotton industry was on the cusp of a sharp decline, and within a few years unemployment would affect thousands of Stockport mill workers like these ones. This is one of just two Stockport films in the Mitchell & Kenyon collection, despite the filmmakers' North West base. | The film was probably shot a day or two ahead of the annual Stockport Fair, which took place on 23 September 1900. Locals would have enjoyed the opportunity to recognise themselves on screen when this film was projected along with many others at the travelling cinematographers' stand.

video_title: Workers at Jesse Crossley & Sons Ironworks, Ripley (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Factories; Women workers; James Crossley and Sons; Ripley | locais: Ripley | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:0wajBtcDogh25qg0VdNzXqLtrLPbEbr8 | Engineering factory gate. | Victorian ironworkers leave their Derbyshire factory in carefully stage-managed fashion. | Mitchell and Kenyon's workplace films are rarely 'pure actuality', as this example makes clearer than most. A showman not only directs the crowd but opens and closes the gate itself. A mass of children patiently await the signal to sweep in front of the camera. The showman gestures to the cameraman that everything's done, but the camera keeps running until the film inside it runs out.
video_title_display: Workers at Jesse Crossley & Sons Ironworks, Ripley (1900)

video_title: Workers at Pilkington Glass Works, St Helens (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Factories; St. Helens; Glass; Child labour; Pilkington Glass Works | locais: St Helens | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:l2aTBtcDqUgn7p98KfukV7tV0x_QH3af | Glass factory gate. | Weary glassmakers head home after morning shift. | Unusually for one of Mitchell and Kenyon's 'factory gate' films, the girls and young women here seem most fascinated by the camera, stopping and staring while their male counterparts hurry past with little more than a slight smile. The exception is a rather grubby, undernourished young lad, who looks old and wizened before his time, who dashes back and forth to make the most of his screen debut.
video_title_display: Workers at Pilkington Glass Works, St Helens (1900)

video_title: Workers at Spring Bank Mill, Nelson (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Women workers; Textile industry; Child labour; Nelson (Lancashire); Spring Bank Mill | locais: Nelson | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:VoaTBtcDrrHbTsmTG_0Cw-3h18RD_Dw6 | Cotton factory gate. | Lively Lancashire millworkers, including young boys and girls, at themill gates. | The practice of employing children to work 'half-time' was widespread in the cotton mills of Lancashire. Here, many of them appear to be in high spirits, though they may have been encouraged by the filmmakers. James Kenyon had a habit of staging colourful incidents for the camera and he - or another showman - may well have prompted some of the rough and tumble action seen here.
video_title_display: Workers at Spring Bank Mill, Nelson (1900)

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Blackburn; Yates Foundry | locais: Blackburn | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:d5aDBtcDpohXKwaeT7tvbNqAe06mxwvT | Note: The factory made boilers for the textile mills and was previously named Foster, Yates and Thom. The building still exists (2003). | A Mitchell and Kenyon film of workers in their home town, intriguing despite (or because of) the poor lighting conditions. | In this early 'factory gate' film it's the gate that dominates, its large form darkening the whole scene. Later films are better composed; here the camera position leaves too little room for the action, with the workers emerging out of the black. But the images are crystal sharp, and there's plenty of energy in the crowd, not least some play-fighting and nose-thumbing from the high-spirited boys. | Mitchell and Kenyon had their base in Blackburn, so it's not surprising that the Lancashire town is well-represented in their films.

video_title: Workforce Leaving Cartwright & Warner Hosiery Works, Loughborough (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Factories; Women workers; Underwear; Loughborough; Cartwright and Warner Hosiery Works | locais: Loughborough | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:Y0OTBtcDpwtgoZMcJjWVmcCLx0QDXBaM | Hosiery factory gate. | Victorian women workers (and a few men) hasten through the gates of the hosiery works after their shift. | There are some strikingly well-dressed women - and a fine array of jackets - among these Loughborough textile workers; the men behind them are not always so smartly turned out. Some 2,000 people worked at this factory in 1900, often enduring long hours and very modest pay. Cartwright and Warner's went out of business in 1929 and parts of the original building have since been converted into flats.
video_title_display: Workforce Leaving Cartwright & Warner Hosiery Works, Loughborough (1900)

video_title: Workforce Leaving Salt's Works in Saltaire (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Yorkshire; Factories; Textile industry; Salts Works; Saltaire | locais: Salts Mill, Saltaire | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:ByODBtcDpx6iHu8QpzQmOmz1Nvg6DQ2z | Workers leaving a textile mill in Saltaire, Yorkshire. | Mill workers swarm into the July sunshine to enjoy a well-earned lunch break. | The cameramen evidently positioned themselves right up the hill to gain a good vantage point from which to capture these workers as they stream out of Salt's Works. The unusually high camera angle distinguishes this film from other Mitchell & Kenyon ‘factory gate' films and allows a good view of the mill's attractive surroundings, and the diverse headwear of these Victorian workers. | This film was shot on 24 July 1900 and ubiquitous advertising in the form of posters and newspaper inserts would have encouraged locals to come and see themselves and their friends and family when the film was screened a few days later at the annual Shipley Feast.
video_title_display: Workforce Leaving Salt's Works in Saltaire (1900)

video_title: Workforce at Hornsby and Sons of Grantham (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Grantham; Child labour; Hornsby's | locais: Grantham | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:pwYTBtcDpJTU5NmLdMK_X8Ih3MggIuIL | Note: Hornsby's was founded in 1815 by Richard Hornsby as a blacksmithing business. It prospered and by 1900 was making agricultural equipment and mobile steam engines used to drive threshing machines and other farm equipment. Hornsby's developed caterpillar tracks for farm vehicles which were later used by tanks in WWI. | It's all sunshine and smiles for the Lincolnshire agricultural equipment makers at home time. | The all-male workforce at this Lincolnshire factory put on a remarkable show of jollity for the filmmakers in this fine early example of Mitchell and Kenyon's many 'factory gate' films. Richard Hornsby had started out as a blacksmith in 1815. By 1900 the company that bore his name was making agricultural equipment and mobile steam engines used to drive threshers and other farm machinery. | Among Hornsby's innovations were caterpillar tracks for farm vehicles, which were later used by tanks in World War I.
video_title_display: Workforce at Hornsby and Sons of Grantham (1900)

video_title: Workforce at Steel & Garland Priory Foundry, Worksop (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Child labour; Priory Foundry; Worksop | locais: Worksop | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:UzYTBtcDrl13BXR9tPSS7F4Wf0-zYEre | An early film showing men and boys leaving a Nottinghamshire metals factory. | This is one of the more orderly workplace exits filmed by Mitchell and Kenyon. Look out, though, for the last three men to leave, good mates in no hurry. Once they're gone the camera is stopped. But as with many of M&K's urban crowd films, the operator then uses what's left of the film stock in the camera to take a second, less tidy shot of kids waving at him - and at us, more than a century on.
video_title_display: Workforce at Steel & Garland Priory Foundry, Worksop (1900)

video_title: Workforce of Brush Electric Co. Falcon Works, Loughborough (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Child labour; Loughborough; Brush Electric Co. | locais: Loughborough | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:ExODBtcDpLbvhYxVD0-Uaorh232LCv_l | Engineering factory gate. | A well-staged exit of workers from a Leicestershire manufacturer for the transport industry. | Filmed in November 1900, this footage was shown at that month's Loughborough town fair. (On the same trip, Mitchell and Kenyon filmed the Cartwright & Warner hosiery works.) Among the duo's 'factory gate' films, this one stands out for its composition, and for the relatively wealthy, well-dressed, well-behaved male crowd steadily snaking past the gate. Note also the profusion of bicycles. | Most of the factories filmed by Mitchell and Kenyon in the early 1900s are long gone, but the Falcon Works site is still in operation today. It remains in the hands of what is now Brush Electrical Machines Ltd, although the company has passed through many owners since its early 20th century days, and now employs several hundred workers where it once had several thousand.
video_title_display: Workforce of Brush Electric Co. Falcon Works, Loughborough (1900)

video_title: Workforce of Howarth's Ordsall Mill, Salford (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Salford; Haworths Ordsall Mill | locais: Salford | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:1zNzBtcDp9ESI11Mjh-1w9J7gp8kzuO6 | Cotton factory gate, related in subject matter to M&K 20. | Workers of all ages emergefrom a Salford mill. | The first workers to spill through the factory gates are the young girls - no doubt determined to make the most of their well-earned time off. The 19th century Factory Acts and Education Acts had helped curtail the worst exploitation of children by factory and mill owners, but the many young faces in this crowd demonstrate that child labour survived into the 1900s.
video_title_display: Workforce of Howarth's Ordsall Mill, Salford (1900)

video_title: Workforce of Ormerod's Mill, Great Moor Street, Bolton (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Women workers; Bolton; Child labour; Omerod's Mill | locais: Great Moor St, Bolton | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:5pNzBtcDp-4ogzUCIBqQsqLCHjNXfs4- | Cotton factory gate. | Eager youngsters burst out of the gates of a Victorian cotton mill. | The lively action more than compensates for this film's grainy images and damaged nitrate stock - although the damage has a strange appeal of its own. The film was shown at an apparently rowdy Bolton New Year Fair: the Bolton Chronicle complained that the festivities “were marred by a vast amount of drunkenness, and unfortunately the gentler sex were not by any means free from the taint.”
video_title_display: Workforce of Ormerod's Mill, Great Moor Street, Bolton (1900)

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Oldham; Platt's New Works | locais: Oldham | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:1sNDBtcDqr7yXOOGYppGfvgXdMi14c40 | Engineering: factory gate. | A visually memorable film of staff exiting a turn-of-the-century Lancashire machine factory. | Teeming workers on a sloping street are shot from a low angle, with strong perspective and a foggy vanishing point, making this a visually striking and rather haunting film. Towards the end, one man lingers in front of the camera. With his direct gaze, inscrutable expression and ethereal presence (he's too close to the camera to stay in focus) he seems to us like a ghost staring at the living. | You have to feel for the boys who also loiter in front of the camera, much more joyously no doubt but, unfortunately, largely below the frameline! The horse, and later a tram, travelling away from the camera add to the film's visual power, while the drinks vendor offering to refresh the exiting workers is an interesting social detail. Over and above their cinematic qualities, Mitchell and Kenyon's 'factory gate' films make fascinating entry points into the late Victorian and early Edwardian economy. As this crowded film suggests, Platts was a large employer. It produced machinery for Lancashire's...

video_title: Workpeople Leaving Dugdale's Paradise Mill, Blackburn (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Cotton (textile); Women workers; Blackburn; Child labour; Paradise Mill | locais: Blackburn | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:9jMTBtcDo1nvmEdglD6ROCX-xPQJSEOo | Dinner time at a textile mill in Victorian Lancashire. | Lancashire communities were built around textile mills and this film, shot in Mitchell and Kenyon's Blackburn base, captures something of the vibrancy of these industrial hubs. The workers' reactions reflect different attitudes towards the still young medium of film. While the boys in the foreground are transfixed by the camera some of the older women are less eager, and even conceal their faces.
video_title_display: Workpeople Leaving Dugdale's Paradise Mill, Blackburn (1900)

video_title: Workpeople Leaving Fish's Waterfall Mill, Blackburn (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Women workers; Blackburn; Fish's Waterfall Mill | locais: Blackburn | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:lzeXlscDrlVqQt4p0j82Att5ERlSXkG1 | Cotton factory gate. | Lancashire cotton workers hurry to lunch aftermorning shift. | A long and exhausting shift in hot, noisy conditions doesn't seem to have quelled the boisterous energy of these mill workers, who push and shove one another as they are funnelled in their hundreds through an archway. While no doubt glad to see the bright sunlight, many squint their eyes, perhaps trying to work out what is going on with the camera operator who is filming in front of them.
video_title_display: Workpeople Leaving Fish's Waterfall Mill, Blackburn (1900)

video_title: Workpeople Leaving Hornby's Brookhouse Mill, Blackburn (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Women workers; Textile workers; Blackburn; Hornby's Brookhouse Mill | locais: Blackburn | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:I0eXlscDpzAFiH-sIEX0QDfNtz30ljJN | Male and female millworkers - young and old - spill out after their shift. | A single shot film, like much of Mitchell and Kenyon's output, this 'factory gate' film shows the workers of a Blackburn mill heading home. Hornby's was one of the few combined spinning and weaving mills in Lancashire. Established in 1828 as a spinning mill, it soon incorporated power loom weaving. Its workforce exceeded 1400 during the 19th century, but it ceased activity in 1932.
video_title_display: Workpeople Leaving Hornby's Brookhouse Mill, Blackburn (1900)

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Factory workers; Cotton (textile); Blackburn; Cats; Child labour; Ordnance Mill | locais: Blackburn | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:Zvd3lscDqz5y3ETqsGsGgFVtK0KI0NRU | Cotton factory gate. | Cheery child workers, averaging around 10 years old, leave the cotton mill after a shift. | It's all smiles as crowds of workers spill out of this Blackburn mill. Many of them appear remarkably young - certainly below 12, the then legal minimum age for the employment of children. This film was advertised in the local press as "Something new for Blackburn - splendid living pictures of workpeople." The mill was built on land formerly belonging to the Board of Ordnance, hence its name.

video_title: Workpeople Leaving Ryland's Mill, Gorton, Manchester (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Women workers; Textile workers; Gorton; Ryland's Mill | locais: Gorton | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:Vnd3lscDpEnzusv73a8IvREb3AXuPmox | Cotton factory gate. | Millworkers bustle home, except for youngsters who are transfixed by the camera. | This film was screened at Gorton Wakes fair in 1900, an event judged the best for some years by The Gorton Reporter, which noted that "a great feature in the fair was the prominence given to 'Living Pictures'... at Cordwell's lifeograph establishment the local picture represented the workpeople leaving Ryland's Mill." John Cordwell was the fairground showman who commissioned this film.

video_title: Workpeople and Girls Leaving Thos. Adams Factory, Nottingham (1900)
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Nottingham; Factory workers; Factories; Women workers; Lace; Thomas Adams Lace Factory | locais: Nottingham | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:U3NDBtcDqKFsx4D0Gy8DkPDQQKF0B3N- | Workers leaving a lace factory gate in Nottingham. | The hustle and bustle of Edwardian lace-workers heading home after a morning shift. | Working conditions at the Quaker-owned Thomas Adams factory were considered better than most in an industry where facilities were basic and temperatures could top 100 degrees Fahrenheit. Lace-production was generally a male preserve, whereas lace-dressing was undertaken by females, hence the large number of women and girls in this film, shot at the Stoney Street finishing warehouse. | Despite long shifts and inadequate ventilation, these employees were relatively fortunate in that their workplace had indoor toilets, a chapel, washing facilities and tea rooms - all very unusual for the time. Adams folded in 1950, after nearly a century of trading. The building is now part of the university college.

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Drama; Western | assuntos: Native North American Indians | locais: Blackburn | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:5obTZtcDqRvBqe45qb3WujCQzTROKG9d | Out-takes of the world's earliest ever 'Western' film. | This appears to be abortive material shot for the film released in 1899 as Kidnapped by Indians. The narrative is baffling as the ethnicity of the costumes is unclear, but seems to concern two girls in a camp who are attacked by 'Red Indians'. The Indians set fire to the camp and a gunfight ensues during which one of the girls is rescued by cowboys. It may be a scene from a well-known show.

Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: London; Fire brigades; Horse-drawn transport; Southwark; Fire engines | locais: Westminster | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:ZuNGU3cjr7U3YQedYRcQorEwQuVrxum4 | FIRE ENGINES TURNING OUT OF THE METROPOLITAN FIRE BRIGADE HEADQUARTERS AT SOUTHWARK BRIDGE ROAD, LONDON. | "London's Burning, London's Burning"... A dynamic early film of horse-drawn fire engines 'turning out' at Southwark Bridge Road. | You can almost hear the clip-clopping hooves and jangling alarm bells in this evocative late Victorian film. It consists of one well-composed shot of three horse-drawn fire engines leaving the Metropolitan Fire Brigade headquarters at Southwark Bridge Road in south London. The entrance to the fire station is shot from an angle, with the camera placed at a sufficient distance to capture the horses running past. Throw in the first fire engine billowing smoke and a crowd of onlookers streaming into the road afterwards, and you end up with one of the more visually dynamic British films of its time. | Fire Brigade demonstrations were popular events, frequently recorded on film at the turn of the century: Edwardian pioneers Mitchell and Kenyon shot several examples.

Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film; Sport | assuntos: Golf; Musselburgh; Park, Willie, Jr. (1864-1925); Fernie, Willie (1928-2011) | locais: Musselburgh | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:swOWp2dToTyi_AybNr9tu0UhHo0bYEhX | Fore! Clear the way for the earliest golf footage from the world's oldest course at Musselburgh. | If you are looking for golf history then Musselbugh Links is the place to start: it's been spoiling good walks since Charles II sat on the throne. This game between Willie Park Jr. and Willie Fernie, both Open winners, was the first of a two-part challenge match with a ?100 prize up for grabs. If the golf swings are perhaps a little unorthodox today, it's nice to see that the celebrations of the players are unchanged. | If you don't want to know the result then look away now - Willie Park was victorious after the second half of the match played at Troon.

video_title: View from an Engine Front - Barnstaple
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Rail transport; Barnstaple | locais: Barnstaple | som: Silent | duração: 2 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:x1eXdzMDE6h83M30wrtMnGQ068fFE3U6 | A phantom ride taken from the buffers of an engine on the L& SW railway; crossing a viaduct in Barnstaple, through Barnstaple station, past a signal box. | A 'phantom ride' along a fondly remembered Devon railway, crossing the distinctive Taw Bridge into Barnstaple on the way to Ilfracombe | Take a lost railway journey into Victorian Devon. As the cameraman clung to the front of a train in 1898, cranking the film by hand, things were looking up for Devon railways. In May the new Barnstaple Town station had opened to share a home with the narrow gauge Lynton and Barnstaple Railway. In the film you can see the older, empty Barnstaple Quay station after crossing the distinctive iron bridge over the River Taw. | To retrace the Ilfracombe Line from Barnstaple today you would have to be on foot or cycle as the line was closed in 1970. The bridge was demolished in 1977, but both stations still stand, repurposed as a café and a sixth form out-building for the nearby Pathfield School.

video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Street scenes; Trams; Horse-drawn transport; Town halls; Bradford | locais: Bradford | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:NtbWFuczoqekJ6kkenCRngxl48K5lvY2 | The earliest surviving footage of bustling Victorian Bradford. | Little is known about this film or who made it - but it's believed to be the earliest surviving footage of Bradford, shot in 1896. The filmmaker picked the perfect vantage point from which to capture the frenetic movement of people and traffic across the square. Look out for the marching band, probably on its way to the nearby St George's Hall, which still hosts Yorkshire brass band competitions today.

video_title: Jack Johnson Paying a Visit to Manchester Docks
video_description: Registro público materializado no BFI Replay. | disponibilidade: Internet-UK | gêneros: Actuality film | assuntos: Dockers; Docks | locais: Salford | som: Silent | duração: 1 min | Brightcove: 6057949427001/ref:5ncmdndTrY83vgBajB9DWrX7wGKLstlE | The legendary world heavyweight boxing champion, John Arthur 'Jack' Johnson, visits Manchester. | The charisma of world heavyweight boxing champion Jack Johnson is palpable in these shots of his visit to Manchester. In the build-up to his fight with British and Empire champion, Bombardier Wells, due to be held at Earl's Court on 2 October 1911, Johnson toured Britain's music halls where his sparring demonstrations proved hugely popular. | It's likely that his tour of Manchester Docks would have coincided with appearances at Manchester's theatres, quite possibly the Regent Theatre, seen here. In the event, under the influence of Methodist minister, Rev. F.B. Meyer, and other clergymen, the Chairman of the London Council prohibited the scheduled fight with Wells on 'religious' grounds, threatening to withdraw Earls Court's licence should it go ahead. The venue's manager reluctantly cancelled the fight but Johnson's music hall tour continued. The conservative clergymen feared that images of a black sporting hero might have a negative impact on the morality of British people.
